_comment: Machine-checkable twin of earth-layer-catalog.svg. The private app
(random-knights/xyz) is the UPSTREAM source of truth for the globe layer
set; this package (xyz-earth) mirrors it. test/layer_catalog_parity_guard_test.dart
enforces this manifest against the package's own EarthAnimatedLayerIds
registry, so a layer can never SILENTLY fall out of parity again (that is
HOW TO KEEP IT CURRENT (same manual-sync model as the score drift guard):
when the app's EarthAnimatedLayerIds changes, update appRegistry below to
match. The guard then forces a decision: either add the layer to the
package (move it into represented + ship its asset/catalog) OR record it
explicitly in pendingPortFromApp with a reason. You cannot record an app
addition and stay green without doing one of the two - that is the point.
The guard is keyless and reads only checked-in files (this repo is PUBLIC
and cannot reach the private app at CI time), so appRegistry is a pinned

represented._note: MUST equal this package's EarthAnimatedLayerIds (flow/scalar/point). The guard asserts set equality per slot.

pendingPortFromApp.reason: Surface wind-speed scalar overlay (|u,v| km/h, storm palette) landed in the app. Not yet ported to xyz-earth: it needs a representative scalar grid asset, a different family from this PR's pointset work. Tracked here so the gap is loud, not silent.

bundledPointAssets._note: point layer id -> its bundled representative/clustered asset. The guard asserts every file exists (rename/delete a bundled asset -> red). satellites has no pointset file (client-propagated TLE orbits), so it is intentionally absent.
